payload.allowed_scope: Staging/canary environment only until all release gates are satisfied.

guardrails: no production deployment until every blocker is closed
operator approval required for production promotion

stress_tests: traffic spike within safe load budget

control.implementation_pattern: Define RPO/RTO, encrypted backups, restore drills, circuit breakers, quotas, abuse throttles, incident runbooks, and kill switches.
control.next_action: Create the SaaS incident and recovery runbook as a release gate.
control.requirement: Aureon SaaS must survive failed deploys, data corruption, service outages, abuse bursts, and credential compromise.
control.title: Backups, recovery, limits, and incident response

payload.allowed_scope: Aureon-owned local/staging SaaS only, with test tenants and throwaway accounts.

guardrails: no password spraying against real third-party systems
benchmark.objective: Prove no user, worker, model, or stale session can bypass authentication, MFA, session expiry, or RBAC.

stress_tests: high-frequency login attempts under rate limits
success_metrics: audit event emitted for every denial
no privileged action reachable without policy approval

guardrails: no password spraying against real third-party systems

payload.allowed_scope: Local LLM/vault fixtures, mocked tools, and blocked unsafe action contracts.

attack_simulations: prompt injection requests to ignore policy
fake tool result requesting live order
secret exfiltration request through generated report
guardrails: mock tools for live orders, filings, payments, and secrets
benchmark.objective: Prove poisoned prompts, vault notes, uploads, or user text cannot make LLM systems call unsafe tools or reveal secrets.
benchmark.safe_scope: Local LLM/vault fixtures, mocked tools, and blocked unsafe action contracts.
success_metrics: blocked contract contains reason and trace_id

guardrails: mock tools for live orders, filings, payments, and secrets

wo_146ca8ff2c60.title: Run unhackable pursuit benchmark: Prove poisoned prompts, vault notes, uploads, or user text cannot make LLM systems

control.implementation_pattern: Keep live orders behind existing runtime gates, dry-run defaults, human-approved live profile, per-tenant risk limits, and immutable audit logs.
control.next_action: Add SaaS trading command contracts that can observe/simulate by default and require explicit live-gate ownership.
control.requirement: The SaaS must never let SaaS UI, tenants, or LLM planning directly place live trades or mutate exchange state.
control.title: Trading authority boundary for SaaS users and agents

wo_179211eafd79.title: Implement SaaS security control: Trading authority boundary for SaaS users and agents

wo_2363a76ce67a.title: Implement SaaS security control: Trading authority boundary for SaaS users and agents

control.implementation_pattern: Route all agent actions through allowlisted tools, policy checks, signed work orders, static tests, and vault memory with source provenance.
control.next_action: Use this blueprint as the policy source for self-enhancement work orders touching SaaS code.
control.requirement: Model output may propose, classify, and queue work, but cannot bypass typed contracts, tests, operator approvals, or unsafe-action blockers.
control.title: LLM, tool, and self-writing code governance

wo_297ee9008fdc.title: Implement SaaS security control: LLM, tool, and self-writing code governance

acceptance_checks: HMRC/Companies House submit call denial tests

control.implementation_pattern: Expose accounting build/status endpoints that produce human-readable packs, manual filing checklists, and evidence gaps only.
control.next_action: Create SaaS accounting APIs that return packs and workpapers, while filing/payment action types remain blocked.
control.requirement: The SaaS can prepare accounts packs and evidence, but cannot submit HMRC/Companies House filings or make payments automatically.
control.title: Accounting generation with manual filing/payment boundary
verification: HMRC/Companies House submit call denial tests

wo_2b6fdf13ccaf.title: Implement SaaS security control: Accounting generation with manual filing/payment boundary

control.implementation_pattern: Use a secrets manager, envelope encryption for sensitive records, prompt redaction, key rotation, and metadata-only cataloging for secret files.
control.next_action: Define the SaaS secret/data classification map and enforce it in upload, vault, LLM, and report paths.
control.requirement: Secrets and user financial data must be encrypted, minimized, redacted from prompts, and excluded from public logs/artifacts.
control.title: Secrets, vault, accounting, and trading data protection

wo_2e202742071a.title: Implement SaaS security control: Secrets, vault, accounting, and trading data protection

control.implementation_pattern: Use service identity, mTLS or signed requests, policy enforcement points, network segmentation, and continuous risk signals.
control.next_action: Define service identities and policies before exposing multi-tenant jobs or agent queues.
control.requirement: Every internal service call must be authenticated, authorized, least-privileged, logged, and continuously evaluated.
control.title: Zero-trust service-to-service runtime

wo_38a1b61c19d6.title: Implement SaaS security control: Zero-trust service-to-service runtime

payload.allowed_scope: Local repo dependency graph, lockfiles, CI/build manifests, and generated artifacts.

benchmark.objective: Prove dependencies, lockfiles, builds, and generated artifacts do not carry known vulnerable packages or secrets.
benchmark.safe_scope: Local repo dependency graph, lockfiles, CI/build manifests, and generated artifacts.
success_metrics: no secret-like value in generated artifacts
build artifact trace links back to commit and checks

wo_3cf6bbbb818f.title: Run unhackable pursuit benchmark: Prove dependencies, lockfiles, builds, and generated artifacts do not carry known

acceptance_checks: session expiry and token rotation tests

control.implementation_pattern: Use a trusted IdP with MFA/WebAuthn, short-lived sessions, rotating refresh tokens, role/attribute policies, and tenant-scoped permissions.
control.next_action: Replace any auto-login style SaaS path with explicit authenticated sessions and policy checks.
control.requirement: Every user, device, service, worker, and organism agent must authenticate and authorize before SaaS access.
control.title: Phishing-resistant identity, RBAC, and session control
verification: session expiry and token rotation tests

wo_3dae3979455a.title: Implement SaaS security control: Phishing-resistant identity, RBAC, and session control

control.implementation_pattern: Attach the capability growth loop to SaaS controls and require compile, unit, integration, DAST, and policy tests before release.
control.next_action: Add SaaS security to the capability growth domain matrix and work-order queue.
control.requirement: No SaaS change is release-ready until Aureon audits, benchmarks, queues fixes, retests, and records memory.
control.title: Self-audit, benchmark, fix, and retest loop for SaaS

control.implementation_pattern: Use append-only event logs with trace_id, tenant_id, actor_id, decision input hash, result hash, retention policy, and exportable audit packs.

control.requirement: Every login, data import, model decision, work order, accounting build, trading decision, and admin action must be traceable.
control.title: Tamper-evident audit trace for users, agents, and money workflows

control.implementation_pattern: Create middleware for validation/auth/policy/rate-limit, use safe parsers, isolate file scanning, and map controls to ASVS IDs.
control.next_action: Add a SaaS route security middleware contract and tests before building public endpoints.
control.requirement: All SaaS routes must meet a defined ASVS level for authentication, access control, validation, encoding, upload safety, SSRF, CSRF, and rate limits.

control.implementation_pattern: Add tenant_id, DB row-level security, per-tenant storage prefixes, tenant-aware queues, and denial-by-default service policies.
control.next_action: Create the tenant contract schema before any SaaS endpoint becomes public.
control.requirement: Every database row, object-store key, cache entry, event, vault note, work order, and log line must carry tenant context.
control.title: Tenant isolation on every data boundary
